# Repository: Infinite Canvas React App
# Purpose: React + TypeScript infinite canvas application
# State Management: Legend State v3 beta

This repository contains a React-based infinite canvas application that enables users to pan, zoom, and draw on an unbounded canvas surface. The app is structured to support multiple tools (pen, eraser), layers, and future extensibility.

Directory Structure:
  ├── index.html           - HTML entry point (root, used by Vite)
  ├── public/              - (optional) static assets (served at '/'), e.g., favicon.ico
  └── src/
      ├── assets/              - static assets imported in code (images, icons, fonts)
      ├── state/               - Legend State v3 beta stores (e.g., canvasStore.ts)
      ├── components/          - reusable UI components
         ├── Canvas/            - core canvas component and drawing helpers
         ├── Toolbar/           - UI controls for drawing tools (pen, eraser, zoom)
         ├── Sidebar/           - optional panels (layers, properties)
         └── common/            - shared UI elements (Buttons, Icons, Modals)
      ├── context/             - React Context providers (CanvasContext)
      ├── hooks/               - custom hooks (usePanZoom, useDraw, useCanvasEvents)
      ├── utils/               - pure helper functions and math operations
      ├── types/               - shared TypeScript type definitions
      └── styles/              - global styles, CSS reset, theming

Configuration:
  package.json             - dependencies and scripts (dev, build, preview)
  tsconfig.json            - TypeScript settings
  .npmrc                   - pnpm config (package-manager)
  pnpm-lock.yaml           - lockfile generated by pnpm
  .gitignore               - ignored files

Development Workflow:
1. Install dependencies: `pnpm install`
2. Start development server: `pnpm dev`
3. Build for production: `pnpm build`
4. Preview production build: `pnpm preview`

This file provides context for language models and new contributors to understand the repository layout and core components.
